goa.overview: India's beach paradise on the western coast. Famous for beaches, Portuguese heritage, fresh seafood, vibrant nightlife, and a laid-back lifestyle unlike anywhere else in India.

north_goa.vibe: Party, backpacker, busy beaches, young crowd

south_goa.vibe: Quiet, luxury, clean beaches, families and couples

panjim.vibe: Heritage, culture, food, cafes, Portuguese architecture

must_eat: Fish Curry Rice (any local shack)
Goan Prawn Curry
Bebinca (traditional Goan dessert)
Xacuti (spiced meat curry)
Choriz Pao (Goan sausage bread)
Rava Fried Fish at Martin's Corner, Betalbatim
Crab Xec Xec
hidden_gems: Butterfly Beach (only accessible by boat from Palolem)
Cabo de Rama Fort (panoramic coastal views)

practical.airport: Goa International Airport, Dabolim (GOI) - 29km from Calangute
practical.railway: Madgaon/Margao Station (South Goa), Thivim (North Goa), Karmali (Panjim)
practical.best_local_transport: Bike/scooter rental ₹350-400/day — absolute best way to explore
practical.sim_card: Jio best coverage, Airtel good too
practical.cash_note: Many beach shacks and taxi drivers prefer cash

seasonal_tips.November: Season just starting. Less crowded, great weather, good deals on hotels.
seasonal_tips.December: Peak season. Book hotels 2 months in advance. Christmas/NYE prices 2-3x normal.
seasonal_tips.January: Best weather. International Film Festival of India in Panjim.
seasonal_tips.February: Goa Carnival (Feb). Excellent weather, moderate crowds.
seasonal_tips.March: End of season, slightly hot afternoons but still great beaches.
seasonal_tips.April: Getting hot and humid. Some shacks closing.
seasonal_tips.May: Very hot, pre-monsoon, avoid beach activities in afternoon.
seasonal_tips.June: Monsoon arrives. Most shacks/water sports closed. Green and beautiful.
seasonal_tips.July: Heavy monsoon. Stunning waterfalls (Dudhsagar best now). Beaches closed.
seasonal_tips.August: Monsoon peaks. Great for Dudhsagar. Food cheap, very few tourists.
seasonal_tips.September: Monsoon easing. Some waterfalls still flowing.
seasonal_tips.October: Post-monsoon. Greenery, fewer crowds, some hotels re-opening.

manali.overview: Himalayan hill station in Himachal Pradesh at 2050m altitude. Gateway to Rohtang Pass, Spiti Valley, and Leh-Ladakh. Famous for snow, adventure sports, Beas River, and stunning mountain scenery.
must_eat: Sidu (local bread with ghee and honey at any dhaba)
Trout fish at Drifters Cafe
Thukpa at Johnson's Cafe
Momos (try Momo Magic near Mall Road)
Himachali Dham (traditional thali)
Apple products — fresh or as juice/cider
famous_spots: Rohtang Pass (permit required — apply online 1 day before)
hidden_gems: Sethan Village (above Manali, snow guaranteed in winter, fewer crowds)
Naggar Castle (15km from Manali, Nicholas Roerich Museum)

practical.best_local_transport: Local taxis or rent a bike for day trips
practical.altitude_note: Altitude 2050m — some people get mild headaches. Rest for first day.
practical.sim_card: BSNL works best in remote areas. Jio works in Manali town.

seasonal_tips.January: Heavy snow. Roads to Rohtang closed. Perfect for snowfall experience.
seasonal_tips.February: Peak winter sports. Cold (-5°C nights). Solang Valley best for snow activities.
seasonal_tips.March: Snow melts slowly. Good for skiing. Roads opening up.
seasonal_tips.April: Excellent for road trips. Snow on peaks, green valleys. Less crowded.
seasonal_tips.May: Peak season. Very crowded (long weekends). Book early. Rohtang opens.
seasonal_tips.June: Great weather, busy. Leh-Manali Highway opens.
seasonal_tips.July: Monsoon — landslide risk on roads. Avoid Rohtang. Manali town OK.
seasonal_tips.August: Landslide risk continues. Stay in Manali town.
seasonal_tips.September: Excellent. Crowds gone, clear skies, beautiful colors.
seasonal_tips.October: Best photography. Clear skies, golden trees. Nights getting cold.
seasonal_tips.November: Snow arriving. Great photography. Some roads may close.

jaipur.overview: The Pink City — capital of Rajasthan. Home to magnificent Mughal and Rajput architecture: Amber Fort, Hawa Mahal, City Palace. Vibrant bazaars, rich culture, and world-class food.
must_eat: Dal Baati Churma (try Chokhi Dhani or Laxmi Misthan Bhandar)
Pyaaz Kachori at LMB (Laxmi Misthan Bhandar)
Lal Maas (fiery red mutton curry)
Ghewar (sweet disc dessert, seasonal)
Mirchi Bada at Rawat Misthan Bhandar
Masala Chai at any roadside stall near Hawa Mahal
famous_spots: Jantar Mantar (largest stone sundial in world)
Jal Mahal (lake palace, view from road)
hidden_gems: Panna Meena Ka Kund (stepwell, photography gem)

practical.airport: Jaipur International Airport (JAI) - 13km from city center
practical.safety: Very safe for tourists including solo women

seasonal_tips.October: Post-monsoon. Green and pleasant. Dussehra celebrations.
seasonal_tips.November: Best month. Pleasant 15-28°C. Diwali period may be busy.
seasonal_tips.December: Pleasant daytime, cold nights. Christmas/NYE tourism picks up.
seasonal_tips.January: Jaipur Literature Festival. Cold mornings. Ideal weather.
seasonal_tips.March: Holi festival — Jaipur has incredible Holi celebrations.
seasonal_tips.May: Very hot 38-44°C. Avoid outdoor sightseeing midday.

kerala.overview: God's Own Country — famous for backwaters (houseboats), tea gardens (Munnar), beaches (Kovalam, Varkala), Ayurveda treatments, and the Kochi cultural hub.
must_eat: Kerala Sadya (traditional feast on banana leaf — try during Onam or at any Kerala traditional restaurant)
Karimeen Pollichathu (pearl spot fish wrapped in banana leaf)
Appam with Stew
Kerala Prawn Curry at any coastal restaurant
Puttu & Kadala Curry (breakfast staple)
Fish Moilee
Banana chips (buy in Thrissur)
famous_spots: Munnar Tea Gardens — sunrise at Echo Point
Fort Kochi (Chinese fishing nets, Jew Town)
hidden_gems: Kumarakom Bird Sanctuary (better than Alleppey for birds)

practical.best_local_transport: Private taxi for hill stations; ferry for backwaters
practical.sim_card: Jio and Airtel both work well
practical.note: Kerala is very safe. Prices slightly higher than other Indian states.

leh_ladakh.overview: High-altitude desert region in the Himalayas at 3500m+. Buddhist monasteries, moonscapes, Pangong Lake, and some of the world's most dramatic mountain roads.
must_eat: Thukpa (noodle soup at any restaurant)
Tibetan Butter Tea (acquired taste — try at a monastery)
Skyu (thick pasta stew)
Chhang (local barley beer, seasonal)
Apricot Jam (local specialty, buy at Leh market)
famous_spots: Pangong Tso Lake (160km from Leh, permit needed)
Khardung La Pass (5359m, one of highest motorable roads)

practical.airport: Kushok Bakula Rimpochhe Airport, Leh (IXL) — daily flights from Delhi (shortest: 1h15m)
practical.acclimatization: MANDATORY: Rest first 2 days in Leh at 3500m. No alcohol first 2 days.
practical.permits: Inner Line Permit needed for Pangong, Nubra. Apply online 2-3 days before.
practical.bike_trip: Manali-Leh (480km, 2 days min, petrol stations scarce — carry extra).
practical.sim_card: BSNL and Jio prepaid work. Airtel limited. No postpaid works.

seasonal_tips.June: Season opens. Roads clear. Pangong accessible. Not too crowded.
seasonal_tips.July: Best weather. Hemis Festival. Slightly crowded.
seasonal_tips.August: Peak season. Independence Day tourism. Full open.
seasonal_tips.September: Excellent. Crowds thinning. Weather still perfect.
seasonal_tips.October: Getting cold. Some passes closing. Last chance before winter.
seasonal_tips.November: Very cold. Most guesthouses closed. Only for hardcore travelers.

delhi.overview: India's capital — a layered city spanning 3000 years of history. From Mughal domes to British bungalows to modern metro. Rich food culture, excellent museums, and world-class street food.
must_eat: Chole Bhature at Sita Ram Diwan Chand (Paharganj)
Paranthe at Paranthe Wali Gali (Chandni Chowk)
Jalebi at Old and Famous Jalebi Wala (Chandni Chowk)
Butter Chicken origin — Moti Mahal, Daryaganj
Streetside Golgappe (Panipuri) — any corner in Lajpat Nagar
Karim's at Jama Masjid (mutton korma, seekh kebab)
Bengali Sweets at Annapoorna (Karol Bagh)

mumbai.overview: The City of Dreams — India's financial capital and entertainment hub. Bollywood, colonial architecture, street food paradise, and a 24/7 energy unlike any other Indian city.
must_eat: Vada Pav — try Ashok Vada Pav (Dadar) or any street stall
Mumbai Misal Pav at Aaswad (Dadar)
Pav Bhaji at Sardar Pav Bhaji (Tardeo)
Bombay Duck (fish) at Mahesh Lunch Home
Kulfi Falooda at Natural Ice Cream
Frankie at Tibb's Frankie
Kebabs at Bade Miyan (behind Taj Mahal Hotel)
famous_spots: Gateway of India + Taj Mahal Palace Hotel

practical.best_local_transport: Local train (CSMT area) — fastest but can be overwhelming. Metro expanding.
practical.safety: Very safe city, even for solo women. Always take Ola/Uber over street cabs at night.

rishikesh.overview: Yoga capital of the world at the foothills of the Himalayas. Gateway to Uttarakhand adventure, white-water rafting, bungee jumping, Beatles Ashram, and sacred Ganga ghats.
must_eat: Thali at Chotiwala Restaurant
Fresh juice at any ghat cafe
Momos at any roadside stall
Banana pancake at Oasis Restaurant
Lassi at Little Buddha Cafe
famous_spots: Laxman Jhula & Ram Jhula (iconic suspension bridges)
Beatles Ashram (Maharishi Mahesh Yogi's abandoned ashram)

agra.overview: Home to the Taj Mahal — one of the world's most iconic monuments. Also has Agra Fort and Fatehpur Sikri. Best visited as a 1-2 day trip from Delhi.
must_eat: Petha (Agra's famous sweet — try Pancchi Petha)
Bedai Poori at Deviram
Mughlai Paratha at any Old City restaurant
famous_spots: Taj Mahal (sunrise visit recommended — least crowded, best light)
Mehtab Bagh (Taj views from opposite side)

practical.transport_from_delhi: Gatimaan Express (fastest, ₹750, 1h40m), Taj Express, Road trip (3-4h from Delhi)
practical.tip: Taj Mahal closed on Fridays. Buy tickets online to skip queues.
practical.air_quality_warning: Winter can have smog. Morning visits best.
